revision.comment: 新しいページ：尾市1号墳（広島県福山市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 尾市1号墳 |画像 = |別名 = 尾市古墳 |所属 = 尾市古墳群 |所在地 = [[広島県]][[福山市]][[新市町]]大字常 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 36|緯度秒 = 11.07 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 16|経度秒 = 46.45 |ISO = JP-34 |形状 = [[八角墳]] |規模 = 直径10.5m |埋葬施設 = [[横口式石槨]] |出土品 = 鉄器・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Hiroshima |アイコン = 八角墳 |ラベル = 尾市1号墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''尾市1号墳'''（おいちいちごうふん、尾市古墳）は、[[広島県]][[福山市]][[新市町]]常にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[八角墳]]。尾市古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。
== 概要 == 広島県東部、[[芦田川]]支流の神谷川に流れ込む芦浦川をさかのぼった、芦浦谷の最奥部の丘陵尾根先端部に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。[[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年）・[[2002年|2002]]-[[2007年]]度（[[平成]]14-19年度）に発掘調査が実施されている。
墳形は八角形で、直径約10.5メートル、高さ1.95メートルを測る{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘周囲には外護列石と見られる石列が巡らされる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。主体部の埋葬施設は[[横口式石槨]]で、南西方向に開口する。三方向に石槨を配置して平面が十字形を呈するという、全国でも類例のない構造の石槨になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。副葬品のほとんどは失われており、調査では若干の鉄器・[[須恵器]]のみが検出されている{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。
この尾市1号墳は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。横口式石槨古墳の多くは畿内に限られるが、備後地方は本古墳のほか[[猪ノ子古墳]]（福山市加茂町下加茂）・[[曽根田白塚古墳]]（福山市芦田町下有地）でも横口式石槨を採用する点で特異性を示しており、その中でも本古墳の場合には畿内にも類例の少ない八角墳である点・複室構造の横口式石槨を有する点で、畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との極めて強い結びつきを示唆するとして注目される古墳になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。
== 埋葬施設 == 主体部の埋葬施設としては[[横口式石槨]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口する。三方向に石槨を配置して平面が十字形を呈するという全国唯一の構造になる。石槨の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 石室全長：主軸6.68メートル、東西4.6メートル * 中央槨：長さ1.61-1.68メートル、奥壁幅1.16メートル、高さ1.15メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 西槨：長さ1.71-1.75メートル、奥壁幅0.95メートル、高さ1.06メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 東槨：長さ1.71-1.79メートル、奥壁幅1.01メートル、高さ1.12メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 羨道：長さ4.06メートル 石槨は[[花崗岩]]の切石を組み合わせることにより、天井・奥壁・側壁・床面は基本的に各1枚で構築される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。石槨内には全面に漆喰が塗布されており、石材の接合部には特に丁寧に施される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。
3石槨のうち中央槨は幅広で短く、西槨・東槨は幅狭で長い構造になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石槨部・羨道部の境では西槨・東槨の南側石端が弧状に面取りされることから、扉石が存在したと推測される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。また羨道部敷石の外には、礼拝石と見られる敷石が据えられる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。
== 関連施設 == * 福山市しんいち歴史民俗博物館・あしな文化財センター（福山市新市町） - 尾市1号墳の模型等を展示。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（新市商工会青年部、1989年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=尾市古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=尾市古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 35 広島県の地名 刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}
== 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1985|chapter=|title=尾市1号古墳発掘調査概報|publisher=新市町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2008|chapter=|title=広島県福山市新市町 尾市第1号古墳発掘調査報告書 -広島県福山市新市町 2002年度（平成14年度）～2007年度（平成19年度）-|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}}
== 関連項目 == <!--{{Commonscat|Oichi Kofun}}--> * [[猪ノ子古墳]] * [[曽根田白塚古墳]]
{{デフォルトソート:おいちこふん}} [[category:広島県の古墳]] [[category:福山市の歴史]] [[category:八角墳]]

羨道：長さ4.06メートル 石槨は[[花崗岩]]の切石を組み合わせることにより、天井・奥壁・側壁・床面は基本的に各1枚で構築される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。石槨内には全面に漆喰が塗布されており、石材の接合部には特に丁寧に施される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 3石槨のうち中央槨は幅広で短く、西槨・東槨は幅狭で長い構造になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石槨部・羨道部の境では西槨・東槨の南側石端が弧状に面取りされることから、扉石が存在したと推測される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。また羨道部敷石の外には、礼拝石と見られる敷石が据えられる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 横口式石槨は単葬が基本であり、3人の合葬を前提として築造された本古墳は異例のものとして評価される{{Sfn|白石太一郎|2007}}。 File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|俯瞰図 File:Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|石槨内部 File:Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|西槨 File:Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|中央槨 File:Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|東槨 File:Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道 == 関連施設 == * 福山市しんいち歴史民俗博物館・あしな文化財センター（福山市新市町） - 尾市1号墳の模型等を展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 尾市1号墳 |画像 = [[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
[[横口式石槨]]開口部 |別名 = 尾市古墳　十字塚　十字古墳 |所属 = 尾市古墳群 |所在地 = [[広島県]][[福山市]][[新市町]]大字常 |位置 = {{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=尾市1号墳}} |形状 = [[八角墳]] |規模 = 直径10.5m |埋葬施設 = [[横口式石槨]] |出土品 = 鉄器・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=尾市1号墳|mark=八角墳.png|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''尾市1号墳'''（おいちいちごうふん、尾市古墳）は、[[広島県]][[福山市]][[新市町]]常にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[八角墳]]。尾市古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 広島県東部、[[芦田川]]支流の神谷川に流れ込む芦浦川をさかのぼった、芦浦谷の最奥部の丘陵尾根先端部に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。[[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年）・[[2002年|2002]]-[[2007年]]度（[[平成]]14-19年度）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は八角形で、直径約10.5メートル、高さ1.95メートルを測る{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘周囲には外護列石と見られる石列が巡らされる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。埋葬施設は[[横口式石槨]]で、南西方向に開口する。三方向に石槨を配置して平面が十字形を呈するという、全国でも類例のない構造の石槨になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。副葬品のほとんどは失われており、調査では若干の鉄器・[[須恵器]]のみが検出されている{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。横口式石槨古墳の多くは畿内に限られるが、備後地方は本古墳のほか[[猪ノ子古墳]]（福山市加茂町下加茂）・[[曽根田白塚古墳]]（福山市芦田町下有地）でも横口式石槨を採用する点で特異性を示しており、その中でも本古墳の場合には畿内にも類例の少ない八角墳である点・複室構造の横口式石槨を有する点で、畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との極めて強い結びつきを示唆するとして注目される古墳になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 === 来歴 === * [[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年）、保存対策目的の発掘調査（新市町教育委員会、[[1985年]]に概報刊行）。 * [[2002年|2002]]-[[2007年]]度（[[平成]]14-19年度）、発掘調査（福山市教育委員会、[[2008年]]に報告書刊行）。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石槨パース図}}]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石槨展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横口式石槨]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口する。三方向に石槨を配置して平面が十字形を呈するという全国唯一の構造になる。石槨の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 石室全長：主軸6.68メートル、東西4.6メートル * 中央槨：長さ1.61-1.68メートル、奥壁幅1.16メートル、高さ1.15メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 西槨：長さ1.71-1.75メートル、奥壁幅0.95メートル、高さ1.06メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 東槨：長さ1.71-1.79メートル、奥壁幅1.01メートル、高さ1.12メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 羨道：長さ4.06メートル 石槨は[[花崗岩]]の切石を組み合わせることにより、天井・奥壁・側壁・床面は基本的に各1枚で構築される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。石槨内には全面に漆喰が塗布されており、石材の接合部には特に丁寧に施される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 3石槨のうち中央槨は幅広で短く、西槨・東槨は幅狭で長い構造になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石槨部・羨道部の境では西槨・東槨の南側石端が弧状に面取りされることから、扉石が存在したと推測される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。また羨道部敷石の外には、礼拝石と見られる敷石が据えられる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 横口式石槨は単葬が基本であり、3人の合葬を前提として築造された本古墳は異例のものとして評価される{{Sfn|白石太一郎|2007}}。 File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|俯瞰図 File:Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|石槨内部 File:Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|西槨 File:Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|中央槨 File:Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|東槨 File:Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道 == 関連施設 == * 福山市しんいち歴史民俗博物館・あしな文化財センター（福山市新市町） - 尾市1号墳の模型等を展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 尾市1号墳 |画像 = [[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
[[横口式石槨]]開口部 |別名 = 尾市古墳/十字塚/十字古墳 |所属 = 尾市古墳群 |所在地 = [[広島県]][[福山市]][[新市町]]大字常 |位置 = {{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=尾市1号墳}} |形状 = [[八角墳]] |規模 = 直径10.5m |埋葬施設 = [[横口式石槨]]（平面十字形） |出土品 = 鉄器・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=尾市1号墳|mark=八角墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''尾市1号墳'''（おいちいちごうふん、尾市古墳）は、[[広島県]][[福山市]][[新市町]]常にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[八角墳]]。尾市古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 広島県東部、[[芦田川]]支流の神谷川に流れ込む芦浦川をさかのぼった、芦浦谷の最奥部の丘陵尾根先端部に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。[[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年）・[[2002年|2002]]-[[2007年]]度（[[平成]]14-19年度）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は八角形で、直径約10.5メートル、高さ1.95メートルを測る{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘周囲には外護列石と見られる石列が巡らされる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。埋葬施設は[[横口式石槨]]で、南西方向に開口する。三方向に石槨を配置して平面が十字形を呈するという、全国でも類例のない構造の石槨になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。副葬品のほとんどは失われており、調査では若干の鉄器・[[須恵器]]のみが検出されている{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。横口式石槨古墳の多くは畿内に限られるが、備後地方は本古墳のほか[[猪ノ子古墳]]（福山市加茂町下加茂）・[[曽根田白塚古墳]]（福山市芦田町下有地）でも横口式石槨を採用する点で特異性を示しており、その中でも本古墳の場合には畿内にも類例の少ない八角墳である点・複室構造の横口式石槨を有する点で、畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との極めて強い結びつきを示唆するとして注目される古墳になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 === 来歴 === * [[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年）、保存対策目的の発掘調査（新市町教育委員会、[[1985年]]に概報刊行）。 * [[2002年|2002]]-[[2007年]]度（[[平成]]14-19年度）、発掘調査（福山市教育委員会、[[2008年]]に報告書刊行）。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石槨パース図}}]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石槨展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横口式石槨]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口する。三方向に石槨を配置して平面が十字形を呈するという全国唯一の構造になる。石槨の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 石室全長：主軸6.68メートル、東西4.6メートル * 中央槨：長さ1.61-1.68メートル、奥壁幅1.16メートル、高さ1.15メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 西槨：長さ1.71-1.75メートル、奥壁幅0.95メートル、高さ1.06メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 東槨：長さ1.71-1.79メートル、奥壁幅1.01メートル、高さ1.12メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 羨道：長さ4.06メートル 石槨は[[花崗岩]]の切石を組み合わせることにより、天井・奥壁・側壁・床面は基本的に各1枚で構築される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。石槨内には全面に漆喰が塗布されており、石材の接合部には特に丁寧に施される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 3石槨のうち中央槨は幅広で短く、西槨・東槨は幅狭で長い構造になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石槨部・羨道部の境では西槨・東槨の南側石端が弧状に面取りされることから、扉石が存在したと推測される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。また羨道部敷石の外には、礼拝石と見られる敷石が据えられる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 横口式石槨は単葬が基本であり、3人の合葬を前提として築造された本古墳は異例のものとして評価される{{Sfn|白石太一郎|2007}}。 File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|俯瞰図 File:Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|石槨内部 File:Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|西槨 File:Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|中央槨 File:Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|東槨 File:Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道 == 関連施設 == * 福山市しんいち歴史民俗博物館・あしな文化財センター（福山市新市町） - 尾市1号墳の模型等を展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == }} '''尾市1号墳'''（おいちいちごうふん、尾市古墳）は、[[広島県]][[福山市]][[新市町]]常にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[八角墳]]。尾市古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 広島県東部、[[芦田川]]支流の神谷川に流れ込む芦浦川をさかのぼった、芦浦谷の最奥部の丘陵尾根先端部に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。[[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年）・[[2002年|2002]]-[[2007年]]度（[[平成]]14-19年度）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は八角形で、直径約10.5メートル、高さ1.95メートルを測る{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘周囲には外護列石と見られる石列が巡らされる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。埋葬施設は[[横口式石槨]]で、南西方向に開口する。3方向に石槨を配置して平面が十字形を呈するという、全国でも類例のない構造の石槨になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。副葬品のほとんどは失われており、調査では若干の鉄器・[[須恵器]]のみが検出されている{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。横口式石槨古墳の多くは畿内に限られるが、備後地方は本古墳のほか[[猪ノ子古墳]]（福山市加茂町下加茂）・[[曽根田白塚古墳]]（福山市芦田町下有地）でも横口式石槨を採用する点で特異性を示しており、その中でも本古墳の場合には畿内にも類例の少ない八角墳である点・複室構造の横口式石槨を有する点で、畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との極めて強い結びつきを示唆するとして注目される古墳になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年）、保存対策目的の発掘調査（新市町教育委員会、[[1985年]]に概報刊行）。 * [[2002年|2002]]-[[2007年]]度（[[平成]]14-19年度）、発掘調査（福山市教育委員会、[[2008年]]に報告書刊行）。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石槨俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石槨展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横口式石槨]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口する。3方向に石槨を配置して平面が十字形を呈するという全国唯一の構造になる。石槨の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 石室全長：主軸6.68メートル、東西4.6メートル * 中央槨：長さ1.61-1.68メートル、奥壁幅1.16メートル、高さ1.15メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 西槨：長さ1.71-1.75メートル、奥壁幅0.95メートル、高さ1.06メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 東槨：長さ1.71-1.79メートル、奥壁幅1.01メートル、高さ1.12メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 羨道：長さ4.06メートル 石槨は[[花崗岩]]の切石を組み合わせることにより、天井・奥壁・側壁・床面は基本的に各1枚で構築される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。石槨内には全面に漆喰が塗布されており、石材の接合部には特に丁寧に施される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 3石槨のうち中央槨は幅広で短く、西槨・東槨は幅狭で長い構造になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石槨部・羨道部の境では西槨・東槨の南側石端が弧状に面取りされることから、扉石が存在したと推測される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。また羨道部敷石の外には、礼拝石とみられる敷石を据える{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 横口式石槨は単葬が基本であり、3人の合葬を前提として築造された本古墳は異例のものとして評価される{{Sfn|白石太一郎|2007}}。 Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|西槨 Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|中央槨 Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|東槨 Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（石槨部方向） == 関連施設 == * 福山市しんいち歴史民俗博物館・あしな文化財センター（福山市新市町） - 尾市1号墳の模型を展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（新市商工会青年部、1989年設置） * {{Cite 刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=白石太一郎|authorlink=白石太一郎|year=2007|chapter=備後の横口式石槨をめぐって|title=大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館館報|issue=11|publisher=[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|白石太一郎|2007}}}} == 関連文献 == 尾市第1号古墳発掘調査報告書 -2002年度（平成14年度）～2007年度（平成19年度）-|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Oichi Kofun}} * [[猪ノ子古墳]] * [[曽根田白塚古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 尾市古墳] - 新市町観光協会 {{デフォルトソート:おいちいちこうふん}} [[category:広島県の古墳]] [[category:福山市の歴史]] [[category:八角墳]]

== 遺跡歴 == * [[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年）、保存対策目的の発掘調査（新市町教育委員会、[[1985年]]に概報刊行）。 * [[2002年|2002]]-[[2007年]]度（[[平成]]14-19年度）、発掘調査（福山市教育委員会、[[2008年]]に報告書刊行）。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石槨俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石槨展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横口式石槨]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口する。3方向に石槨を配置して平面が十字形を呈するという全国唯一の構造になる。石槨の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 石室全長：主軸6.68メートル、東西4.6メートル * 中央槨：長さ1.61-1.68メートル、奥壁幅1.16メートル、高さ1.15メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 西槨：長さ1.71-1.75メートル、奥壁幅0.95メートル、高さ1.06メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 東槨：長さ1.71-1.79メートル、奥壁幅1.01メートル、高さ1.12メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 羨道：長さ4.06メートル 石槨は[[花崗岩]]の切石を組み合わせることにより、天井・奥壁・側壁・床面は基本的に各1枚で構築される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。石槨内には全面に漆喰が塗布されており、石材の接合部には特に丁寧に施される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 3石槨のうち中央槨は幅広で短く、西槨・東槨は幅狭で長い構造になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石槨部・羨道部の境では西槨・東槨の南側石端が弧状に面取りされることから、扉石が存在したと推測される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。また羨道部敷石の外には、礼拝石とみられる敷石を据える{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 横口式石槨は単葬が基本であり、3人の合葬を前提として築造された本古墳は異例のものとして評価される{{Sfn|白石太一郎|2007}}。 Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|西槨 Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|中央槨 Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|東槨 Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（石槨部方向） == 関連施設 == * 福山市しんいち歴史民俗博物館・あしな文化財センター（福山市新市町） - 尾市1号墳の模型を展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（新市商工会青年部、1989年設置） * {{Cite }} '''尾市1号墳'''（おいちいちごうふん、尾市古墳）は、[[広島県]][[福山市]][[新市町]]常にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[八角墳]]。尾市古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 広島県東部、[[芦田川]]支流の神谷川に流れ込む芦浦川をさかのぼった、芦浦谷の最奥部の丘陵尾根先端部に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。[[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年）・[[2002年|2002]]-[[2007年]]度（[[平成]]14-19年度）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は八角形で、直径約10.5メートル、高さ1.95メートルを測る{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘周囲には外護列石と見られる石列が巡らされる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。埋葬施設は[[横口式石槨]]で、南西方向に開口する。3方向に石槨を配置して平面が十字形を呈するという、全国でも類例のない構造の石槨になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。副葬品のほとんどは失われており、調査では若干の鉄器・[[須恵器]]のみが検出されている{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。横口式石槨古墳の多くは畿内に限られるが、備後地方は本古墳のほか[[猪ノ子古墳]]（福山市加茂町下加茂）・[[曽根田白塚古墳]]（福山市芦田町下有地）でも横口式石槨を採用する点で特異性を示しており、その中でも本古墳の場合には畿内にも類例の少ない八角墳である点・複室構造の横口式石槨を有する点で、畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との極めて強い結びつきを示唆するとして注目される古墳になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 埋葬施設としては[[横口式石槨]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口する。3方向に石槨を配置して平面が十字形を呈するという全国唯一の構造になる。石槨の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 石室全長：主軸6.68メートル、東西4.6メートル * 中央槨：長さ1.61-1.68メートル、奥壁幅1.16メートル、高さ1.15メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 西槨：長さ1.71-1.75メートル、奥壁幅0.95メートル、高さ1.06メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 東槨：長さ1.71-1.79メートル、奥壁幅1.01メートル、高さ1.12メートル{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 羨道：長さ4.06メートル 石槨は[[花崗岩]]の切石を組み合わせることにより、天井・奥壁・側壁・床面は基本的に各1枚で構築される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。石槨内には全面に漆喰が塗布されており、石材の接合部には特に丁寧に施される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 3石槨のうち中央槨は幅広で短く、西槨・東槨は幅狭で長い構造になる{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石槨部・羨道部の境では西槨・東槨の南側石端が弧状に面取りされることから、扉石が存在したと推測される{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。また羨道部敷石の外には、礼拝石とみられる敷石を据える{{Sfn|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 横口式石槨は単葬が基本であり、3人の合葬を前提として築造された本古墳は異例のものとして評価される{{Sfn|白石太一郎|2007}}。 Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|西槨 Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|中央槨 Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|東槨 Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（石槨部方向） == 関連施設 == * 福山市しんいち歴史民俗博物館・あしな文化財センター（福山市新市町） - 尾市1号墳の模型を展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（新市商工会青年部、1989年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=尾市古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|尾市古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=尾市第1号古墳|title=新市町史|volume=資料編I|publisher=広島県芦品郡新市町|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新市町史 資料編I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=尾市古墳|title=広島県の地名 刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|尾市古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=白石太一郎|authorlink=白石太一郎|year=2007|chapter=備後の横口式石槨をめぐって|title=大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館館報|issue=11|publisher=[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|白石太一郎|2007}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1985|chapter=|title=尾市1号古墳発掘調査概報|publisher=新市町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2008|chapter=|title=広島県福山市新市町 尾市第1号古墳発掘調査報告書 -2002年度（平成14年度）～2007年度（平成19年度）-|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Oichi Kofun}} * [[猪ノ子古墳]] * [[曽根田白塚古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 尾市古墳] - 新市町観光協会 {{デフォルトソート:おいちいちこうふん}} [[category:広島県の古墳]] [[category:福山市の歴史]] [[category:八角墳]]

{{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Ancient Japanese Tumulus |Name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |Image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |OtherName=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |Coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |Shape={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * **1984:** Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * **2002–2007:** Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * **Total chamber length:** Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * **Central chamber:** Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * **Western chamber:** Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * **Eastern chamber:** Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * **Passage (Sen-dō):** Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * **Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center** (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * **Historical Marker:** Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place Names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[ja:尾市1号墳]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |OtherName=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |Coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |Shape={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * **1984:** Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * **2002–2007:** Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * **Total chamber length:** Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * **Central chamber:** Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * **Western chamber:** Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * **Eastern chamber:** Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * **Passage (Sen-dō):** Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * **Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center** (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * **Historical Marker:** Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place Names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Ancient Japanese Tumulus |Name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |OtherName=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * **1984:** Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * **2002–2007:** Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * **Total chamber length:** Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * **Central chamber:** Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * **Western chamber:** Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * **Eastern chamber:** Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * **Passage (Sen-dō):** Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * **Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center** (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place Names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi revision.text:
{{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |Name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |OtherName=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place Names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On revision.text:
{{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |Name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |OtherName=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place Names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:尾市1号墳]] {{draft categories| {{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |Name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |OtherName=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place Names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |Name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |OtherName=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place Names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |Name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |OtherName=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place Names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association
