revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Oichi No.1 Kofun]] to [[Oichi Kofun]]
revision.text: #REDIRECT [[Oichi Kofun]]

{{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:尾市1号墳]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:attempting to force Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association revision.text:
{{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] pages]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] 2024]][[Category:Oichi No. 1 Kofun]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association revision.comment: Bumping for adding the Kofun template
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] to [[Category:kkkk]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association {{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} {{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style SeCategoryaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style SeCategoryaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style SeCategoryaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style SeCategoryaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style SeCategoryaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style SeCategoryaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, seCategoryaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, seCategoryaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, seCategoryaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, {{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun,
