Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Oichi No.1 Kofun]][[en:draft:Oichi No.1 Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Oichi No.1 Kofun|Oichi No.1 Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}{{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Oichi No.1 Kofun]][[en:draft:Oichi No.1 Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Oichi No.1 Kofun|Oichi No.1 Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun |Group=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} |Scale=Diameter: 10.5m |BurialFacility={{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |Artifacts=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |AccompanyingTombs= |ConstructionPeriod=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |BuriedPerson= |ImperialMausoleum= |HistoricSite=None |TangibleCulturalProperty= |SpecialNotes= |Map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not designated as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59年}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal shape, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments forming a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial artifacts have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal shape and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == Burial Facility == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} with the following dimensions:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina Cultural Property Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links
