revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun | part_of=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |map_size=Diameter: 10.5m |burial={{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |AccompanyingTombs= |built=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred_individual= |ImperialMausoleum= |Historic Site=None |TangibleHistoric Site= |Specialnotes= |map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|map_dot_label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59th year }} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == burial == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} with the following area:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun | part_of=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |map_size=Diameter: 10.5m |burial={{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |AccompanyingTombs= |built=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred_individual= |ImperialMausoleum= |Historic Site=None |TangibleHistoric Site= |Specialnotes= |map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|map_dot_label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59th year }} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == burial == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} with the following area:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎}}|year=2007|chapter=On Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} == External Links Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun | part_of=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市|12=draft}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=draft}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} |map_size=Diameter: 10.5m |burial={{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} |AccompanyingTombs= |built=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Interred_individual= |ImperialMausoleum= |Historic Site=None |TangibleHistoric Site= |Specialnotes= |map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|map_dot_label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59th year|12=draft}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=draft}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == burial == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with the following area:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎|12=draft}}|year=2007|chapter=On Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association {{draft categories| [[ceb:Oichi No.1 Kofun]] }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown Entrance to the {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun | part_of=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市|12=draft}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=draft}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} |map_size=Diameter: 10.5m |burial={{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} |AccompanyingTombs= |built=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Interred_individual= |ImperialMausoleum= |Historic Site=None |TangibleHistoric Site= |Specialnotes= |map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|map_dot_label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59th year|12=draft}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=draft}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == burial == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with the following area:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=draft}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == burial == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with the following area:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Entrance to the {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun | part_of=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市|12=draft}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=draft}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} |map_size=Diameter: 10.5m |burial={{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} |AccompanyingTombs= |built=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Interred_individual= |ImperialMausoleum= |Historic Site=None |TangibleHistoric Site= |Specialnotes= |map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|map_dot_label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59th year|12=draft}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=draft}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == burial == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with the following area:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Entrance to the {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun | part_of=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市|12=draft}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=draft}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} |map_size=Diameter: 10.5m |burial={{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} |AccompanyingTombs= |built=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Interred_individual= |ImperialMausoleum= |Historic Site=None |TangibleHistoric Site= |Specialnotes= |map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|map_dot_label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59th year|12=draft}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=draft}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == burial == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with the following area:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=draft}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == burial == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with the following area:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun | part_of=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市|12=draft}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=draft}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} |map_size=Diameter: 10.5m |burial={{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} |AccompanyingTombs= |built=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Interred_individual= |ImperialMausoleum= |Historic Site=None |TangibleHistoric Site= |Specialnotes= |map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|map_dot_label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59th year|12=draft}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=draft}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == burial == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with the following area:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎|12=draft}}|year=2007|chapter=On Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association {{draft categories| [[ceb:Oichi No.1 Kofun]] }} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oichi No.1 {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun | part_of=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市|12=draft}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=draft}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} |map_size=Diameter: 10.5m |burial={{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} |AccompanyingTombs= |built=Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Interred_individual= |ImperialMausoleum= |Historic Site=None |TangibleHistoric Site= |Specialnotes= |map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|map_dot_label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|ja|昭和59th year|12=draft}} and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=draft}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == burial == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with the following area:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎|12=draft}}|year=2007|chapter=On Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association {{draft categories| [[ceb:Oichi No.1 Kofun]] }} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oichi No.1 It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} and belongs to the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=draft}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingdom|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == burial == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} with the following area:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1
