So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=[[File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun | part_of=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location=[[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]] {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|qq=}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size=Diameter: 10.5m |burial={{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] |subsidiary sites= |built=Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred_individual= |ImperialMausoleum= |Historic Site=None |TangibleHistoric Site= |Specialnotes= |map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|map_dot_label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1984|Showa 59]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == burial == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Encyclopedia|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place names|qq=}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Taichirō|qq=}}|year=2007|chapter=On Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun | part_of=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location=[[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]] {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|qq=}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size=Diameter: 10.5m |burial={{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] |subsidiary sites= |built=Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred_individual= |ImperialMausoleum= |Historic Site=None |TangibleHistoric Site= |Specialnotes= |map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|map_dot_label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1984|Showa 59]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == burial == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Encyclopedia|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place names|qq=}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Taichirō|qq=}}|year=2007|chapter=On Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - Entrance to the {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun | part_of=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location=[[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]] {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|qq=}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size=Diameter: 10.5m |burial={{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] |subsidiary sites= |built=Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred_individual= |ImperialMausoleum= |Historic Site=None |TangibleHistoric Site= |Specialnotes= |map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|map_dot_label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1984|Showa 59]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == burial == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Encyclopedia|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place names|qq=}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Taichirō|qq=}}|year=2007|chapter=On Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=21 Oichi Kofun] - |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|11.07|N|133|16|46.45|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Chamber|qq=}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] |subsidiary sites= |built=Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred_individual= redirect}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }} '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1984|Showa 59]] and during the years 2002–2007.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} == Excavation History == * '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008. == burial == [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}} Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}} Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) == Related Facilities == * '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by '''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|qq=|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.
== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1984|Showa 59]] and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>
<gallery> Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==

'''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|qq=|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.
== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1984|Showa 59]] and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>
<gallery> Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) </gallery>

== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>

'''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.
== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1984|Showa 59]] and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.

The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>

The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>
<gallery> Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) </gallery>

'''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.
== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1984|Showa 59]] and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>

The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>

'''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.
== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1984|Showa 59]] and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>

== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1984|Showa 59]] and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>

== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1984|Showa 59]] and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>

The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.
== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1984|Showa 59]] and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>
<gallery> Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun.
