'''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.
== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1984|Showa 59]] and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>
<gallery> Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) </gallery>

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun | part_of=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location=[[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]] {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34.60308333333333|N|133.27958333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size=Diameter: 10.5m |burial={{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] |subsidiary sites= |built=Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred_individual= |ImperialMausoleum= |Historic Site=None |TangibleHistoric Site= |Specialnotes= |map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|map_dot_label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }}
'''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.
== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1984|Showa 59]] and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>
<gallery> Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun.

The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. == burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]

'''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.
== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1984|Showa 59]] and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>
<gallery> Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun.

'''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.
== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1984|Showa 59]] and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingdom]].<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>
<gallery> Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun.

'''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.

== Overview ==

Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=Showa 59}} and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>

The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>

The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingdom}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>

== Excavation History ==

* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985).
* '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.

== burial ==

[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]]
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]

The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
* '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters
* '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref>
* '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref>
* '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref>
* '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters

The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>

Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>

<gallery>
Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber
Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber
Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber
Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance)
Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber)
</gallery>

== Related Facilities ==

* '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oichi Kofun]][[ja:尾市1号墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oichi Kofun]][[ja:尾市1号墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oichi Kofun]][[ja:尾市1号墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun | part_of=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}} {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34.60308333333333|N|133.27958333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size=Diameter: 10.5m |burial={{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} |subsidiary sites= |built=Late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} |Interred_individual= |ImperialMausoleum= |Historic Site=None |TangibleHistoric Site= |Specialnotes= |map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|map_dot_label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }}
'''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.
== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=Showa 59}} and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingdom}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>
<gallery> Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) </gallery>

[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oichi Kofun]][[ja:尾市1号墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oichi Kofun]][[ja:尾市1号墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oichi Kofun]][[ja:尾市1号墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun | part_of=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}} {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34.60308333333333|N|133.27958333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size=Diameter: 10.5m |burial={{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} |subsidiary sites= |built=Late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} |Interred_individual= |ImperialMausoleum= |Historic Site=None |TangibleHistoric Site= |Specialnotes= |map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|map_dot_label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }}
'''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.
== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=Showa 59}} and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingdom}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>
<gallery> Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun.

== Bibliography == * '''Historical Marker:''' Installed by Shin'ichi Chamber of Commerce Youth Division, 1989. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koichi Sato|year=1989|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title={{ill|Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Encyclopedia|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|title=Shin'ichi Town History (Volume I, Resources)|publisher=Shin'ichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shin'ichi Town History Volume I|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Oichi Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture: Later Editions ({{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place names|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Series)|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichirō|ja|白石太一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Taichirō|1=Taichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580156}}|year=2007|chapter=On Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku in Bigo Region|title=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin (Issue 11)|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}

== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oichi Kofun]][[ja:尾市1号墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oichi Kofun]][[ja:尾市1号墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oichi Kofun]][[ja:尾市1号墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun | part_of=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}} {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34.60308333333333|N|133.27958333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size=Diameter: 10.5m |burial={{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} |subsidiary sites= |built=Late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} |Interred_individual= |ImperialMausoleum= |Historic Site=None |TangibleHistoric Site= |Specialnotes= |map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|map_dot_label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }}
'''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.
== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=Showa 59|1=1984}} and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingdom|1=Yamato Kingship}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>
<gallery> Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun.

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}

[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]

'''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.
== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=Showa 59|1=1984}} and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingdom|1=Yamato Kingship}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>
<gallery> Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) </gallery>

'''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.
== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=Showa 59|1=1984}} and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingdom|1=Yamato Kingship}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>
<gallery> Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) </gallery>

[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Oichi No. 1 Kofun |image=File:Oichi Kofun, entrance.jpg |Othername=Oichi Kofun / Jūjizuka / Jūji Kofun | part_of=Oichi Kofun Cluster |location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}} {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}} {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, Tsumane |coordinates={{Coord|34.60308333333333|N|133.27958333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size=Diameter: 10.5m |burial={{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} (cross-shaped floor plan) |burial goods=Iron tools, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} |subsidiary sites= |built=Late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} |Interred_individual= |ImperialMausoleum= |Historic Site=None |TangibleHistoric Site= |Specialnotes= |map={{location map|Japan Hiroshima|map_dot_label=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|omitted=circle}} }}
'''Oichi No. 1 Kofun''' (尾市1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shin'ichi Town|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'ichi Town|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}}, {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and a part of the Oichi Kofun Cluster. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a Historic Site.
== Overview ==
Situated in eastern Hiroshima, the tomb is located on the ridge of a hill at the uppermost part of the Ashiuradani Valley, which branches off from the Kamitani River, a tributary of the Ashida River.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=Showa 59|1=1984}} and during the years 2002–2007.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb has an octagonal type, measuring approximately 10.5 meters in diameter and 1.95 meters in height. A ring of stones, interpreted as an outer protective stone circle, surrounds the mound.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with a cross-shaped floor plan, opening to the southwest. The layout, featuring three compartments typeing a cross, is unique in Japan.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> Most burial burial goods have been lost, with only fragments of iron tools and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} recovered during excavations.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} and belongs to the {{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|lt=Late Kofun tumuli|lt=End of the Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The use of {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} is rare outside the Kinai region, but the Bigo area (modern Fukuyama) is notable for adopting this feature in tombs such as the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The unique characteristics of Oichi No. 1 Kofun, including its octagonal type and cross-shaped stone chamber, suggest a strong connection to the Kinai-based {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingdom|1=Yamato Kingship}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
== Excavation History ==
* '''1984:''' Excavation for preservation purposes (Shin'ichi Town Board of Education, summary report published in 1985). * '''2002–2007:''' Excavation by Fukuyama City Board of Education, report published in 2008.
== burial ==
[[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-B2-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Oichi Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Expanded view of the stone chamber]]
The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} with the following area:<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> * '''Total chamber length:''' Main axis 6.68 meters; East-West 4.6 meters * '''Central chamber:''' Length 1.61–1.68 meters, rear wall width 1.16 meters, height 1.15 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Western chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.75 meters, rear wall width 0.95 meters, height 1.06 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Eastern chamber:''' Length 1.71–1.79 meters, rear wall width 1.01 meters, height 1.12 meters<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}</ref> * '''Passage (Sen-dō):''' Length 4.06 meters
The chambers were constructed using cut granite, with each wall, ceiling, and floor made from a single stone slab.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref> The interior was entirely coated with plaster, with particular attention given to sealing joints between stones.<ref>{{Harvid|Oichi Kofun (Heibonsha, Later Edition)|2006}}</ref>
Unusually, the tomb was built to accommodate three burials, a deviation from the standard single-interment practice of {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichirō|2007}}</ref>
<gallery> Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Western chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Central chamber Oichi Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Eastern chamber Oichi Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Oichi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward stone chamber) </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* '''Shin'ichi History and Folklore Museum / Ashina designation1 Center''' (Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama): Features a model of Oichi No. 1 Kofun.
